experiments.name: Typecheck
experiments.detail: Exit code 2. 100+ type errors present across campaign/*, test/*, storage/, tool/, script/build.ts. None are attributable to trust-boundary changes — all errors are in pre-existing modules (campaign/auditor.ts, campaign/binder.ts, test/capability-proxy-bisect.test.ts, etc.). Zero type errors in trust-boundary files (enforcer.ts, hook-dispatch-guard.ts, types.ts, auth.ts, oauth-provider.ts, xai.ts).

experiments.name: isValidCapabilityId runtime guard
experiments.detail: isValidCapabilityId defined as type guard using capabilityValuesSet.has(value). Const array CapabilityIdValues provides runtime values. Branded type CapabilityId = (typeof CapabilityId)[keyof typeof CapabilityId]. Guard invoked at hook-dispatch-guard.ts:52 before capability check. If invalid ID passed, returns Effect.succeed(false) — safe failure.

experiments.name: Per-entry auth decoding
experiments.detail: decodeAuthDataPerEntry() iterates each entry individually via Schema.decodeUnknownOption(Entry)(value). Valid entries land in valid Record; failed entries land in corrupted string[]. Falls back to empty {} on null/non-object input. Prevents one corrupted entry from rejecting all others.

experiments.name: Corrupted auth backup file creation
experiments.detail: When corrupted.length > 0, creates timestamped backup at mcp-auth.json.corrupted.<timestamp>. Backs up raw data (including corrupted entries) via fs.writeJson with 0o600 permissions. Logs via Effect.logError with backupPath, corruptedKeys, timestamp. Comment claims SHA256 hash logged but actual code logs corruptedKeys (string array of entry keys) — not SHA256. Functional behavior correct; comment is misleading.
experiments.observation: Comment at line 37-38 says 'SHA256 hash logged for forensic analysis' but code logs corruptedKeys (key names, not SHA256). Minor documentation mismatch.

experiments.name: persistCrash/persistSuccess log errors
experiments.detail: persistCrash at line 413: Effect.logError('persistCrash: failed to persist crash state', { pluginId, error, errorType: typeof error }). persistSuccess at line 429: same pattern. Both catch blocks now explicitly log errors with structured context (pluginId, error, errorType). Previously these were silent Effect.catch(() => Effect.void).

evidence.after: Effect.catch((error) => Effect.logError('...', { pluginId, error, errorType: typeof error }))

experiments.name: enforcer.ts uses Effect.gen pattern
experiments.detail: Refactored from Effect.flatMap(registry.get(pluginId), (state) => ...) with manual Effect.succeed() calls to Effect.gen(function* () { const state = yield* registry.get(pluginId); ... }). Added structured Effect.logWarning calls for diagnostic logging on quarantine ('Capability check denied: plugin quarantined') and capability denial ('Capability check denied: capability not granted').

experiments.name: as any removals in plugin/index.ts
experiments.detail: Removed: (1) 'secrets.access' as any → CapabilityId.SecretsAccess, (2) stripSecrets(cfg as any) as any → stripSecrets(cfg), (3) dispatchGuard call as any → direct type. Added comment on line 441: 'Deferred: Hooks intersection type has no index signature for dynamic hook dispatch.' Remaining as any casts: 5 pre-existing (lines 338, 371, 379, 471, 487). Notably, line 487's checkCapability(s.capabilityRegistry, pluginId, capability as any) bypasses the branded CapabilityId type — see defect D-3 below.
experiments.defect_detail: See defect D-3: checkCapabilityFn at line 487 accepts capability: string and casts as any, bypassing CapabilityId branded type.

experiments.name: xai.ts Schema validation
experiments.detail: Added DeviceCodeResponseSchema = Schema.Struct({ device_code: Schema.String, user_code: Schema.String, verification_uri: Schema.String, verification_uri_complete: Schema.optional(Schema.String), expires_in: Schema.optional(Schema.Number), interval: Schema.optional(Schema.Number) }). Replaced manual (await response.json()) as DeviceCodeResponse + manual field validation with Schema.decodeUnknownSync(DeviceCodeResponseSchema)(json). Removed as DeviceCodeResponse unsafe cast. 8 remaining providers deferred as claimed.

experiments.name: Secret redaction in oauth-provider.ts (boolean flags)
experiments.detail: Changed log.info('saved dynamically registered client', { mcpName, clientId: info.client_id }) to log.info('saved dynamically registered client', { mcpName, hasClientId: !!info.client_id }). Actual client_id value no longer logged; replaced with boolean hasClientId flag. Other log calls (saveTokens, tokens, etc.) were not modified — they were already not logging token values (only mcpName and error objects).

defects.title: Zero test coverage for all trust-boundary specific functions
defects.detail: No tests exist for: isValidCapabilityId(), decodeAuthDataPerEntry(), persistCrash(), persistSuccess(), HookDispatchGuard.shouldDispatch(), DeviceCodeResponseSchema validation. All 6 trust-boundary functions have 0% test coverage. This means no automated verification that the fixes work or remain working.
defects.recommendation: Add unit tests for each trust-boundary function before declaring done.

defects.title: Auth backup comment claims SHA256 hash but code logs key names
defects.detail: auth.ts:37 comment says 'SHA256 hash logged for forensic analysis'. Actual code at lines 83-97 logs corruptedKeys (array of string key names), not SHA256 hashes. No cryptographic hash is computed.

defects.title: Public Plugin.checkCapability API bypasses CapabilityId branded type
defects.detail: Plugin.Interface.checkCapability is typed as (pluginId: string, capability: string) => Effect.Effect<boolean>. The implementation at line 487 casts capability as any before passing to the enforcer's checkCapability which requires CapabilityId. This means any external caller can pass an arbitrary string, bypassing the isValidCapabilityId guard. The guard only protects internal hook dispatch (HookDispatchGuard), not the public Plugin.checkCapability API.
defects.recommendation: Either change the Interface type to require CapabilityId, or add isValidCapabilityId() validation inside checkCapabilityFn before delegating.

defects.title: fix-E claim count mismatch
defects.detail: Claim states '5 catch blocks logged'. Actual new catch block logging found: 3 (persistCrash, persistSuccess, xai.ts JWT parse). The diff also shows added Effect.logWarning in enforcer.ts (not catch blocks) and pre-existing catch+log patterns in oauth-provider.ts (not new).

defects.title: 5 remaining as any casts beyond the 2 deferred
defects.detail: Claim says 'deferred rationale for remaining 2'. Code has 7 total as any in plugin/index.ts: 2 were removed (secrets.access, cfg), 2 deferred with rationale comment (hook index signature at lines 441, 451), leaving 5 remaining (lines 338, 371, 379, 471, 487) that were pre-existing but are not acknowledged in the claim.

defects.title: oauth-provider.ts catch blocks still log raw error objects
defects.detail: All log.error calls in oauth-provider.ts pass the raw error object: log.error('failed to save tokens', { mcpName, error }). Error messages could contain token data in stack traces or message strings. Only the clientId → hasClientId change was made; token-related error logging was not addressed.

admission_recommendation.reasoning: All 6 fully-implemented fixes (A, C, D, G, C5, C6) are correctly implemented and the production code paths are well-guarded. The 2 partially-implemented fixes (B, E) match their stated scope. However, D-1 (zero test coverage) and D-3 (public API bypasses branded type) are blocking-quality defects that should be addressed. D-2, D-4, D-5, D-6 are documentation/minor issues that don't block admission.
minimum_actions_before_merge: Add unit test for isValidCapabilityId() covering valid IDs, invalid IDs, empty string
Add unit test for decodeAuthDataPerEntry() covering mixed valid/corrupted, all valid, all corrupted entries
Fix D-3: validate capability string with isValidCapabilityId() in checkCapabilityFn at line 487, or change Interface type to accept CapabilityId
